'''Bandonia''' was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="Scum" /> located in the [[Bandonian system]]. It was a part of the [[Arrowhead]], or [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> Bandonia shared its name with the [[Disease/Legends|disease]] known as the [[Bandonian Plague]].<ref name="Wraith">''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]''</ref>

The [[Tark family estate|estate]] of the family of<ref name="GG10" /> the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Boes Tark]] was located at Bandonia, and Tark's [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|son]], [[Moxin Tark|Moxin]],<ref name="WbC">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']]</ref> who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]],<ref name="23 BBY">[[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']] is dated to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]. Therefore, the events of ''Wanted by Cracken'' translate to [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. In addition, ''Wanted by Cracken'' states [[Moxin Tark]] is thirty years old. He must have therefore been [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]].</ref> grew up there.<ref name="WbC" /> When he was [[5 BBY/Legends|eighteen years]] old, however, Moxin Tark fled his family's estate, eventually becoming a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]].<ref name="GG10" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bandonia was first mentioned in [[Louis J. Prosperi]]'s [[1993]] [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' sourcebook [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']].<ref name="WbC" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Bandonian [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square L-10.<ref name="TEA" />
